name: Tallinn (Estonia)
description: A white field charged with three vertical blue rectangles centred along the vertical axis, derived from the colours of the medieval Tallinn coat of arms.
history: Tallinn's flag, in white and blue, references the city's historic coat of arms (a white cross on red), reinterpreted in the late 20th century. The three-rectangle design was adopted by the Tallinn City Council in modern Estonia.

features.symbolism: Three vertical blue rectangles on a white field, recalling the city's historic civic colours.
